name: Zürich Horn Playground
description: A spectacular adventure playground located directly on the shores of Lake Zürich. Features large wooden climbing structures, slides, rope bridges, a water play area with dams (great for warm days), swings, and plenty of space for parents to relax on the grass.

comments.author: Sarah M.
comments.text: Excellent water play zone. My 4yo could play here for hours! Very clean and nice lake breeze.

comments.author: Beat R.
comments.text: Very crowded on sunny weekends, but otherwise the best park in Zürich.

comments.author: Elena S.
comments.text: Amazing views of the lake while the kids climb. Toilets are nearby and stroller access is great.

name: FIFA Museum - Kids Game Zone
description: An indoor interactive playground located inside the FIFA Museum. Features physical football simulator games, arcade style panels, giant pinball, drawing stations, and physical skill-testing areas. Perfect for rainy days in Zürich.

comments.author: Thomas K.
comments.text: Perfect rainy day activity. The interactive giant pinball game is a massive hit!

comments.author: Anna L.
comments.text: Highly recommend for energetic kids. It's safe and keeps them moving.

name: Alpamare Indoor Water Park
description: Europe's largest covered water park. Features 12 water slides (totaling 2.1km of sliding fun!), indoor and outdoor pools, wave pool, toddler splash paddling zone with mini slides, and beautiful views of Lake Zurich.

comments.author: Sandra G.
comments.text: The toddler splash area is brilliant! Heated water, safe and warm. Older sibling loved the thrill slides.

name: Gurten Playground & Toboggan
description: Situated on Bern's home mountain, Gurten. An enormous playground featuring a miniature train system, climbing grids, giant wood slides, water streams for splashing, a kids car driving circuit, and a spectacular summer toboggan run.

comments.author: Christof M.
comments.text: Best playground in Switzerland, bar none. Kids train rides are super cheap. Amazing BBQ spots around.

name: Bernaqua Water Park & Wave Pool
description: A huge, futuristic indoor water park in Bern Westside. Offers a jungle wave pool, three large indoor slides with lights and sound, a wild river stream pool, and a dedicated, warm baby pool with interactive toys.

comments.author: Verena W.
comments.text: Very modern slides! The baby zone is warm and has lots of fountains.

name: Parc des Bastions Playground
description: A historic park in the heart of Geneva. Features a shaded kids' playground with sand pits, climbing towers, and wooden structures, plus giant chess sets that kids love. Fenced area makes it easy to monitor toddlers.

comments.author: Jean P.
comments.text: Fenced in, which is amazing. Nice cafe nearby for parents. Easy walk from Geneva Old Town.

name: Yatouland Indoor Fun Park
description: A large indoor leisure arena in Geneva designed for kids. Features multiple bouncy castles, giant inflatables, a soft play obstacle course, a laser tag arena, and a mini trampoline section. Excellent for active energy burn.

comments.author: Sophie T.
comments.text: My kids were absolutely exhausted after 2 hours of jumping. Very safe setup.

name: Park im Grünen & Dinosaur Park
description: A huge park near Basel featuring a fantastic wooden adventure playground, life-sized static dinosaur figures, a miniature lake with ducks, carousel rides, and a mini golf course. Extremely family-friendly with large picnic lawns.

comments.author: Lukas H.
comments.text: One of the best free places around Basel. The giant wooden slides and dyno structures are cool.

name: Aquabasilea Splash Pool
description: A premier water park in the Basel area. Includes a rapid outdoor river torrent, several high-thrill slides, an indoor wave pool, and a kids water village area equipped with spray nozzles, buckets, and low slides.

comments.author: Katja S.
comments.text: Excellent slides and safe paddling pool. Can get crowded in winter.

name: Lido Luzern Beach & Pool
description: A gorgeous lake beach resort on Lake Lucerne. Offers a 250-meter long sandy beach, a large heated outdoor kids pool with fountains, water toys, an adventure playground, and vast grassy lawns for sunbathing.
fees: 7 CHF for Adults, 3 CHF for Kids

comments.author: Markus F.
comments.text: Perfect summer spot. The sand beach is great for toddlers to build castles, and pool is clean.

name: Swiss Transport Museum Roadwork Zone
description: An interactive, educational kid zone inside the museum. Children can drive miniature dump trucks, operate diggers, load soft bricks, and build roads wearing high-visibility vests. A wonderful tactile experience.

comments.author: Daniela K.
comments.text: My 5yo spent 2 hours playing road constructor. Outstanding interactive area!

name: Signal de Bougy Park
description: A huge, free-access recreational park overlooking Lake Geneva. Contains large wooden play castles, ziplines, swings, a petting zoo with goats and pigs, a mini-golf course, and an amphitheater with kids' performances.

comments.author: Julien V.
comments.text: Unbelievable park. Pets corner is fantastic for small children and the park views are beautiful.

name: Splash & Spa Tamaro
description: A tropical-themed indoor water park in Ticino. Boasts a massive kids' splash paradise under glass domes, multiple wave generators, low-hazard spray parks, and slide networks. Heated year-round to 34°C.

comments.author: Giovanni B.
comments.text: Excellent indoor temperature. Perfect for winter days when it's raining or snowing outside.

name: Lido di Lugano Swimming Pool
description: Classic outdoor resort featuring an Olympic pool, diving towers, a dedicated heated kids' pool with mini-fountains and a slide, plus a grass playground area right next to the lake.
fees: 8 CHF for Adults, 4 CHF for Kids

comments.author: Monica M.
comments.text: Clean water, beautiful backdrop. Good playground area next to the toddler pool.

name: St. Gallen Wildlife & Playpark Peter & Paul
description: A nature-themed forest park. Features a lovely woodland kids playground, walkable pathways where children can spot ibexes, deer, marmots, and wild boars. Very peaceful and educational.

comments.author: Rita Z.
comments.text: Walking trails are stroller-friendly. Kids loved seeing the baby deer up close!

name: Allmendhubel Flower Park
description: One of the most iconic alpine playgrounds in Switzerland, featuring a water labyrinth, flower trail, wood zip line, climbing frames, and wooden alpine cows, all with the Eiger, Mönch, and Jungfrau peaks as a backdrop.

comments.author: Peter S.
comments.text: Unbelievable views. Best playground experience ever.

name: Mooraculum Nature Playpark
description: A massive nature adventure park focused on moorland ecosystems. Contains obstacle courses, suspended wooden walkways, a water play station with rafts, sound games, and a educational trail for curious minds.

comments.author: Elena G.
comments.text: Excellent water play! Bring dry clothes for the kids.

name: Wolli's Adventure Park at Sunnegga
description: Located next to the scenic Leisee lake, this playground features wooden play boats, a water raft that kids can pull across the lake, climbing frames, slides, and sand pits with a direct view of the Matterhorn.

comments.author: Marc F.
comments.text: Fabulous swimming lake and play area right in front of the Matterhorn.

name: Bort Alpine Playground Grindelwald
description: Nesting on Grindelwald-First slopes, this playground covers 700m2 with slides, balancing beams, climbing ropes, trampolines, and stream beds for water games, all with views of the Eiger north face.

comments.author: Nadia T.
comments.text: Excellent setup for children. Very clean and safe with rubber floorings.

name: Kiddy Dome Indoor Adventure Park
description: One of Switzerland's largest indoor playgrounds. Features multi-level climbing labyrinths, vertical drop slides, donut-glider tracks, interactive play zones, jumping trampolines, and a dedicated toddler soft play section.

comments.author: Bruno P.
comments.text: Massive place. Kids absolutely love it. Good seating area for parents.

name: Trampolino Indoor Funpark
description: Known as the largest children's indoor playground in Switzerland. Packed with high-thrill trampolines, a giant climbing volcano, climbing walls, a mini ferris wheel, indoor go-karts, and a massive soft maze.

comments.author: Stefan W.
comments.text: A bit loud, but the trampoline park selection is fantastic.

name: Spiez Indoor Funpark
description: An indoor play paradise with over 2000m² of space. Features bouncy castles, soft obstacles, climbing networks, table football, air hockey, and safe baby zones for the smallest kids.

comments.author: Tanja M.
comments.text: Great place, reasonably priced. The baby section is well padded and clean.

name: Urba Kids Indoor Adventure
description: A multi-level indoor play center located in Orbe. Boasts colorful bouncy castles, soft ball pools, electric mini-karts, rope slides, and interactive games. Organizes treasure hunts during winter.

comments.author: David G.
comments.text: Excellent for cold and rainy afternoons. Very friendly staff.

name: Leukerbad Therme Family Zone
description: The largest thermal baths alpine resort in Europe. Incorporates warm indoor and outdoor pools, two giant high-speed loop slides, and a dedicated kids splash paradise filled with water cannons and nozzles.

comments.author: Pascal O.
comments.text: Splashing in warm thermal water while snow covers the mountains outside is a magic feeling.

name: Baar Lättich Indoor & Outdoor Pool
description: A comprehensive family pool facility. Offers a 105-meter giant water slide, an indoor lazy river, heated wellness pools, kids' splash play yards, and massive outdoor swimming bays during the summer season.

comments.author: Esther B.
comments.text: The 105m slide is super fun. Outdoor lawn is very wide and green.

name: MFO Park Climbing Pergola
description: An architectural marvel in Zurich. The entire structure is a giant steel double-walled framework covered in green vines. Features integrated climbing stairs, skywalks, and lookouts that older kids love to climb and explore.

comments.author: Jonas T.
comments.text: A very unique space. Excellent for older children who love to climb. No swings, but great design.

name: Irchelpark Nature Play Spaces
description: A beautifully designed natural park in Zurich. Features vast green slopes, wide paved walking tracks, small wooden bridges over natural streams, duck ponds, and gravel playing zones ideal for outdoor active play.

comments.author: Anna F.
comments.text: A wonderful place to run around and see birds. Highly recommend bringing a picnic.

name: Strandbad Zug Kids Beach & Pool
description: A family-favorite lakeside beach on Lake Zug. Offers a flat, gently sloping sandy beach, a shaded baby paddling pool, a water trampoline, and a lovely kids playground on the lawns.
fees: 4 CHF for adults, 2 CHF for kids

comments.author: Leo G.
comments.text: Great sand and very calm water. Perfect beach pool for toddlers.

name: Marzili Outdoor Swimming Pools
description: Bern's famous riverside swimming spot. Provides huge, free-access grassy lawns, multiple children wading pools with fountains and animal sprinklers, a 50m lap pool, and a giant water slide.

comments.author: Klaus M.
comments.text: Can't believe this beautiful pool facility is completely free. Toddler fountains are fantastic.

name: Play Village Zurich
description: A premium, beautifully crafted indoor play space where children engage in role-playing. Features miniature towns with a post office, supermarket, veterinarian clinic, and kitchen, letting kids imagine and play.

comments.author: Maya D.
comments.text: Excellent for imaginative kids. High quality wooden toys and roleplay sets.

name: Schluefweg Swimming Complex Kloten
description: A fantastic swimming complex for the whole family in Kloten. Features a spectacular outdoor pool with a giant 'rocket ship' water slide, kids splash paddling zone under sun protection, a 5m diving tower, and a year-round indoor pool with a warm 30°C non-swimmer area.

comments.author: Marcus F.
comments.text: The rocket ship slide is absolutely amazing for kids! Very neat facilities and helpful staff.

name: Entdeckerspielplatz Egetswil
description: An outdoor adventure discovery playground in Egetswil, Kloten. Features wood-built climbing units, balance beams, swings, a water-stream play system with dams, and dedicated fireplace/grill installations for family BBQs.

comments.author: Julia H.
comments.text: Excellent water play system. Make sure to bring a change of clothes! Very clean and quiet.

name: Seilpark Zürich (Forest Ropes Course)
description: An outdoor high ropes adventure course located in the forest next to Zentrum Schluefweg. Features 11 challenging trails of varying heights and difficulty, including kid-friendly trails with continuous safety belays. A perfect active family day out.

comments.author: Oliver G.
comments.text: Highly secure safety harness system for the children. Friendly instructors. Great physical challenge!

name: Stadtpark Playground Kloten
description: A modern public playground located in the heart of Kloten Stadtpark near the Altbach stream. Offers custom wooden climbing towers, multi-lane slides, swings, and sandboxes in a nicely shaded green environment.

comments.author: Renata S.
comments.text: Beautiful park environment. Central, clean, and lots of different playsets.

name: Zurichhorn Park & Playground
description: Beloved lakeside park with splash areas, picnic meadows, and great views of Lake Zurich. Free entry, perfect for all ages.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Bellerivepark
description: Charming lakefront park with water play features, swings, and open green spaces for children to run free.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Elfenau Park
description: One of Bern's most beautiful playgrounds set in trees and meadows, with water features, slides, a wooden fort, sandbox, and swings.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: St. Johanns Park
description: Well-equipped family park with play facilities, clean toilets, lockers, nursing room, and a splash pool for little ones.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Parc La Grange
description: Geneva's most popular family park with a rose garden, large playground, open lawns by Lake Geneva, and seasonal water jets.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Bois de la Bâtie
description: Popular woodland park with a small zoo, adventure playground, mini train, and mini-golf — a full day out for families.
address: Route de la Forêt, 1213 Onex, Geneva

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Parc de Sauvabelin
description: Enchanting forest park with a deer enclosure, lake, wooden tower, and nature playground — magical for children year-round.
address: Chemin du Bois de Sauvabelin, 1018 Lausanne

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Inseli Park
description: Scenic lakeside park in central Lucerne with a great playground, paddleboat rentals nearby, and breathtaking mountain views.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Zugersee Lakeside Park
description: Gorgeous lake park with a children's playground, paddling area, seasonal beach, and views of the Alps across Lake Zug.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Stadtpark St. Gallen
description: Central city park with a large playground, pond, wooded pathways, and open lawns great for family picnics.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Stoos Alpine Playground
description: Spectacular car-free alpine village with a fenced adventure playground at 1300m altitude, with stunning lake and mountain views.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Alpamare Water Park
description: Switzerland's largest water park! 12 slides spanning 2.1 km, children's paradise, wave pool, and wellness. 30 min from Zurich by train.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Dolder Bad
description: Historic lido in leafy grounds above Zurich. 50m outdoor pool, shaded paddling pool, and play area surrounded by forest.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Bernaqua Adventure Pool
description: The largest adventure pool in Canton Bern — 2,000 m² of water, three giant slides, white-water canyon, and 18 different pools.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Marzili Freibad
description: Among Switzerland's oldest baths, right in the shadow of the Parliament. Free entry, river swim on the Aare, massively popular in summer.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Weyermannshaus Freibad
description: Switzerland's largest outdoor pool by water surface — 16,000 m² across multiple pools with waterslides and a children's area.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Genève-Plage
description: Iconic lakeside lido on Lake Geneva with an Olympic pool, beach area, water slides, and stunning views of Mont Blanc.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Piscine de Bellerive
description: Outdoor lakeside pool complex with a 50m competition pool, children's pool, grass sunbathing areas, and Lake Geneva access.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Lido Lucerne
description: Beautiful outdoor pool and beach on Lake Lucerne with mountain backdrop. Lakeside swimming, pools for all ages, and a stunning Alpine setting.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Lido di Lugano
description: Mediterranean-feel lido in Ticino with lake swimming, waterslides, sandy beach area, and palm trees — a taste of the Italian south.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Flip Lab Zurich
description: Massive 3,000 m² trampoline park near Zurich airport with jump zones, dodge ball, foam pit, parkour, and trained instructors.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Play Village Zürich
description: Creative indoor play world with themed rooms — supermarket, café, construction site, medical centre. Book a 2-hour slot. Ages 1–8.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: The Jump Spot Lausanne
description: Switzerland's biggest indoor trampoline park — 150 trampolines across 3,300 m²! Foam pits, slam dunk zones, dodgeball, and ninja course.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Jump Factory Basel
description: Huge trampoline park with basketball trampolines, foam pit, free jump areas, and dodgeball court — great for all ages.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Kids Arena Lucerne
description: Lucerne's largest indoor playground for ages 0–10. Climbing wall, ball pit, electric go-kart track, trampolines, and a soft-play toddler zone.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Freiruum Spielruum — Zug
description: Indoor playground for children aged 2–7 inside the Freiruum complex, plus a 120 m² trampoline park. Café on site.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Elevation Indoor Winterthur
description: Creative indoor playground in Winterthur's Lagerplatz district — bouncy castles, swirly slides, trampolines, and a ball pit.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: Kindercity Bern
description: Interactive children's city where kids role-play as doctors, firefighters, chefs, and more in themed rooms for imaginative play.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.

name: X-Stream Trampoline Park
description: High-energy trampoline park east of Zurich with a ninja warrior course, slam dunk zones, foam pits, and dedicated toddler sections.

comments.author: SwissKids Explorer
comments.text: Featured premium spot imported from our curated directory.
